subject: Physics
title: Frequency and Pitch
description: A 60-second lesson on Frequency and Pitch.

hook.text: A piccolo plays high notes and a bass drum plays low ones. The difference is how fast the air vibrates.

buildup.text: Frequency measures vibrations per second, in hertz. More vibrations mean a higher pitch.

discovery.text: Humans hear from 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz. Below 20 Hz is infrasound; above 20,000 Hz is ultrasound.

twist.text: As you age, you lose high-frequency hearing first. Most adults can't hear above 15,000 Hz.

climax.text: Concert A is standardized at 440 Hz. Every orchestra on Earth tunes to this frequency.

punchline.text: Fast vibrations sound high. Slow vibrations sound low. That's pitch.

quiz.question: What is the range of human hearing?
